_meta.description: Per-field documentation for the generation endpoints (POST /txt2img, /img2img, /txt2vid, /upscale). Sourced from sd.cpp (include/stable-diffusion.h sd_img_gen_params_t, sd_vid_gen_params_t, sd_sample_params_t, sd_guidance_params_t, sd_slg_params_t, sd_cache_params_t, sd_pm_params_t, sd_hires_params_t).

prompt.label: Prompt
prompt.description: Text prompt fed to the model's text encoder (CLIP / T5 / LLM). Supports inline `<lora:name:weight>` tags — these are auto-extracted into the LoRA list before being sent to the encoder. Supports a1111-style dynamic-prompts (`{a|b|c}`) when `expand_prompt: true` is set.

negative_prompt.label: Negative Prompt
negative_prompt.description: What to push the model away from. Has effect only when `cfg_scale > 1` (CFG is what compares cond vs uncond). Ignored on architectures that don't use CFG (most flow-matching models default to `cfg_scale = 1`).
negative_prompt.recommended: Optional. Empty is a fine default for Flux / SD3 / Z-Image.

width.label: Width (px)
width.description: Output image width in pixels. Must be divisible by the model's patch size (typically 8 or 16). Architectures have native resolutions they were trained at — going far off them can degrade quality.
width.recommended: Match the architecture's training resolution: SD1.5=512, SDXL=1024, Flux/SD3/Z-Image=1024, Wan video=832.

height.label: Height (px)
height.description: Output image height in pixels. Same divisibility constraint as `width`.
height.recommended: Match training resolution. For non-square: total pixel count close to native is more important than aspect ratio.

steps.label: Sampling Steps
steps.description: Number of denoising steps the sampler runs. More steps = closer to the model's converged output, with diminishing returns. Distilled models (Flux Schnell, SDXL Turbo, Z-Image Turbo) need only 4–8.
steps.recommended: 20–30 for SD1.5/SDXL, 20 for Flux Dev, 4–8 for *-Turbo or Schnell variants.

cfg_scale.label: CFG Scale
cfg_scale.description: Classifier-Free Guidance scale. Strength of pushing the cond toward the prompt vs the uncond. Higher = follows prompt more aggressively but can over-saturate. Set to 1.0 to disable CFG (skips the uncond pass — twice as fast).
cfg_scale.recommended: SD1.5: 7. SDXL: 4–8. Flux Dev: 1 (CFG bypassed in flow models). Z-Image: 1. Schnell/Turbo: 1.

distilled_guidance.label: Distilled Guidance
distilled_guidance.description: Distilled-CFG scale for models that bake the CFG behavior into the diffusion model itself (Flux). Replaces the runtime CFG split.
distilled_guidance.recommended: Flux Dev: 3.5 (sd.cpp default). Other architectures: ignored.

eta.label: Eta
eta.description: Stochasticity parameter for DDIM-family samplers. 0 = deterministic, 1 = max stochasticity (matches DDPM noise schedule). Most other samplers ignore this.
eta.recommended: 0 (deterministic, reproducible). Raise only with DDIM-trailing for some variation.

shifted_timestep.label: Shifted Timestep
shifted_timestep.description: Start the sampling schedule from a non-final timestep (used by NitroFusion and similar fast-sampling fine-tunes). 0 = standard schedule. 250–500 = NitroFusion's range.
shifted_timestep.recommended: 0 unless you're explicitly running a NitroFusion-style fine-tune.

seed.label: Seed
seed.description: RNG seed for the initial noise tensor (and stochastic samplers). -1 = pick a random one each generation. Same seed + same prompt + same model = same image.
seed.recommended: -1 for variety. Pin a specific number for A/B comparing prompt or sampler changes.

sampler.label: Sampler
sampler.description: Sampling algorithm. Different samplers can produce different images at the same seed; quality and speed differ too.

values.euler_a: Euler ancestral — adds noise each step (less reproducible, more variety)
values.lcm: LCM — for LCM-finetuned models (4–8 step generation)
values.ddim_trailing: DDIM trailing — required for some fine-tunes

scheduler.label: Scheduler
scheduler.description: Determines the noise schedule (the timesteps the sampler walks through). Pairs with the sampler — some combinations (e.g. karras+dpmpp2m) are well-tested, others may be off.

values.karras: Karras — concentrates more steps near the end, common for SDXL
values.simple: Simple — used by Flux / SD3 flow models

scheduler.recommended: discrete or karras for SD1.5/SDXL; simple for Flux/SD3; smoothstep for Z-Image.

batch_count.label: Batch Count
batch_count.description: Number of independent images to produce in this single job. Each image gets a different seed (`seed`, `seed+1`, …). Total VRAM doesn't grow with batch_count — sd.cpp serializes.
batch_count.recommended: 1 for interactive, higher when you want a grid of variations from one prompt.

clip_skip.label: CLIP Skip
clip_skip.description: Skip the last N layers of CLIP when encoding the prompt. -1 = use the model's recommended default. 2 is the classic anime-model setting.
clip_skip.recommended: -1 (auto). Set to 2 for anime/cartoon SD1.5 fine-tunes.

flow_shift.label: Flow Shift
flow_shift.description: Per-generation flow-matching shift parameter for Flux / SD3 / Z-Image models. Higher values shift sampling toward larger noise levels longer. Per-call override; the load-time `flow_shift` is the default if this isn't set.

slg_scale.label: SLG Scale
slg_scale.description: Skip Layer Guidance scale. Selectively zeros out a few diffusion layers when computing the unconditional pass — improves anatomy / coherence on SD3.5-medium and similar. 0 = disabled.
slg_scale.recommended: 0 for most models. 2.5 for SD3.5 Medium with the recommended skip layers.

skip_layers.label: SLG Skip Layers
skip_layers.description: Which transformer layers to skip during the SLG unconditional pass. SD3.5 Medium uses [7, 8, 9].
skip_layers.recommended: [7,8,9] for SD3.5 Medium. Other models: leave default, ignored when slg_scale=0.

slg_start.label: SLG Start (% of steps)
slg_start.description: Fraction of the sampler schedule (0.0–1.0) at which SLG kicks in.

slg_end.label: SLG End (% of steps)
slg_end.description: Fraction of the sampler schedule at which SLG turns off.

custom_sigmas.label: Custom Sigma Schedule
custom_sigmas.description: Replace the scheduler's noise sigma sequence with a hand-tuned one. Bypasses `scheduler`. Empty array = use the chosen scheduler.
custom_sigmas.recommended: Empty unless you're hand-tuning per-step noise levels — niche.

ref_images.label: Reference Images (base64)
ref_images.description: Base64-encoded reference images for Flux Kontext / image-edit. Each image gets encoded into the conditioner alongside the text prompt.
ref_images.recommended: Use for Flux Kontext or models that accept reference images.

auto_resize_ref_image.label: Auto-resize Ref Images
auto_resize_ref_image.description: Resize each reference image to the output `width × height` before encoding.
auto_resize_ref_image.recommended: On unless your refs are already exactly the target size.

increase_ref_index.label: Increase Ref Index
increase_ref_index.description: Increment the per-ref index counter inside the model's encoder, signaling 'these references should be treated as later in a sequence'. Niche; check your model's docs.

control_image_base64.label: ControlNet Image (base64)
control_image_base64.description: Base64-encoded pre-processed control image. Must match the ControlNet model loaded — Canny edges for canny model, depth map for depth model, etc. sd.cpp does not pre-process; you do that client-side.

control_strength.label: ControlNet Strength
control_strength.description: How much the ControlNet influences the diffusion process (0.0–1.0). Lower = looser following, higher = tighter following at cost of overall coherence.
control_strength.recommended: 0.6–0.9 for most uses. Lower for creative prompts where control should be a hint, not a constraint.

vae_tiling.label: VAE Tiling
vae_tiling.description: Per-generation override of the model-load `vae_tiling`. Process VAE encode/decode in tiles to reduce peak VRAM.
vae_tiling.recommended: Enable for ≥2048 px outputs. Otherwise leave to the load-time default.

vae_tile_size_x.label: VAE Tile Width
vae_tile_size_x.description: Width of VAE tiles when tiling is on. 0 = use load-time default.

vae_tile_size_y.label: VAE Tile Height
vae_tile_size_y.description: Height of VAE tiles. 0 = use load-time default.

vae_tile_overlap.label: VAE Tile Overlap
vae_tile_overlap.description: Overlap fraction between VAE tiles for seam blending.

cache_mode.label: Cache Mode
cache_mode.description: DiT-model intermediate caching strategy. Skips redundant computation across consecutive sampler steps when the model's intermediate state hasn't changed enough to matter. `easycache` is the simplest; `spectrum` is the newest, generally best for Flux/SD3/Z-Image.
cache_mode.recommended: spectrum for Flux/SD3/Z-Image when generation is too slow. Off for SD1.5/SDXL (UNet is too small for caching to win).

easycache_threshold.label: EasyCache Threshold
easycache_threshold.description: Reuse threshold for EasyCache. Higher = more aggressive reuse (faster but more quality loss). Range typically 0.05–0.4.
easycache_threshold.recommended: 0.2 starting point. Tune up for more speed, down for more quality.

easycache_start.label: EasyCache Start (% of steps)
easycache_start.description: Fraction of steps before EasyCache becomes active. Small to skip the early high-noise steps where caching hurts.

easycache_end.label: EasyCache End (% of steps)
easycache_end.description: Fraction of steps after which EasyCache turns off (last few steps fully recomputed for sharpness).

spectrum_w.label: Spectrum Cache: w
spectrum_w.description: Spectrum-cache `w` weight (frequency cutoff). Higher = retains more spectrum, less speedup.
spectrum_w.recommended: 0.5 default. See sd.cpp PR #1322 for tuning.

spectrum_window_size.label: Spectrum Cache: window size
spectrum_window_size.description: Spectrum-cache analysis window size in steps.

spectrum_warmup_steps.label: Spectrum Cache: warmup steps
spectrum_warmup_steps.description: Steps at the start of sampling before spectrum cache becomes active.

spectrum_stop_percent.label: Spectrum Cache: stop percent
spectrum_stop_percent.description: Fraction of steps after which spectrum cache disengages (last steps recomputed).

pm_id_images.label: PhotoMaker ID Images (base64)
pm_id_images.description: Reference images for PhotoMaker identity preservation. Multiple faces of the same person.
pm_id_images.recommended: 3–5 images of the same person from different angles when using PhotoMaker.

pm_id_embed_path.label: PhotoMaker ID Embed Path
pm_id_embed_path.description: Server-side path to a pre-computed PhotoMaker ID embedding. Skips per-generation ID encoding.
pm_id_embed_path.recommended: Empty unless you've pre-computed an embedding for repeated use.

pm_style_strength.label: PhotoMaker Style Strength
pm_style_strength.description: PhotoMaker style-vs-identity tradeoff. Higher = more style influence, less identity preservation.
pm_style_strength.recommended: 20 (sd.cpp default). Drop to 10 for stronger identity preservation.

upscale.label: Upscale After Generate
upscale.description: Run the loaded ESRGAN upscaler on the output image after generation. Requires an upscaler to be loaded via POST /upscaler/load.
upscale.recommended: On for one-shot 'generate then upscale' workflows.

upscale_repeats.label: Upscale Repeats
upscale_repeats.description: Number of post-generation auto-upscale passes (each with the upscaler's native factor — 4× ESRGAN × 2 passes = 16×). DISTINCT from the `/upscale` endpoint's own `repeats` field, which controls passes inside a single upscale job. `upscale_repeats` only chains additional /upscale calls after txt2img / img2img finish.
upscale_repeats.recommended: 1. Two passes amplify artifacts, but produces large outputs from small inputs.

upscale_auto_unload.label: Auto-unload Upscaler
upscale_auto_unload.description: Free upscaler VRAM right after the upscale step. Useful when you generated with `upscale: true` and want VRAM back for other work.

expand_prompt.label: Expand Prompt Template
expand_prompt.description: If true, parse `prompt` for a1111-style dynamic-prompts syntax (`{a|b|c}`, `{N$$a|b|c}`) and create one queue item per variation. The response then carries `group_id` + `variation_count` + `job_ids[]` instead of a single `job_id`. Hard cap: 200 variations per request.
expand_prompt.recommended: Enable when your prompt actually contains `{…}` syntax.

init_image_base64.label: Init Image (base64)
init_image_base64.description: Base64-encoded source image for img2img / image-edit. Required for /img2img.

mask_image_base64.label: Mask Image (base64)
mask_image_base64.description: Base64-encoded inpainting mask. White areas are repainted, black areas preserved.
mask_image_base64.recommended: Optional. Empty mask = standard img2img (no inpainting).

strength.label: Denoising Strength
strength.description: How much of the init image's noise to keep (0.0 = identical to init, 1.0 = ignore init entirely). Controls how aggressively img2img diverges from the input.
strength.recommended: 0.5–0.75 for natural-looking edits. 0.9+ for radical reinterpretation.

img_cfg_scale.label: Image CFG Scale
img_cfg_scale.description: Image-CFG for instruct-pix2pix-style models. -1 = same as `cfg_scale`. Different from `cfg_scale`: balances text-prompt influence against image-prompt influence.

video_frames.label: Video Frames
video_frames.description: Number of frames to generate (Wan / video models).

fps.label: Output FPS
fps.description: Frames per second baked into the resulting MP4. Doesn't affect generation, just the encoded video's playback rate.

high_noise_steps.label: High-noise Steps (MoE)
high_noise_steps.description: For Wan 2.2 MoE: how many steps the high-noise expert runs before handing off to the low-noise expert. 0 = let the model decide via `moe_boundary`.

high_noise_cfg_scale.label: High-noise CFG (MoE)
high_noise_cfg_scale.description: CFG scale used during the high-noise expert phase of Wan 2.2 MoE.

high_noise_distilled_guidance.label: High-noise Distilled Guidance
high_noise_distilled_guidance.description: Distilled-guidance during the high-noise expert phase (Wan 2.2 MoE).

high_noise_sampler.label: High-noise Sampler
high_noise_sampler.description: Sampler to use during the high-noise expert phase.

high_noise_slg_scale.label: High-noise SLG Scale
high_noise_slg_scale.description: SLG scale for the high-noise expert phase.

high_noise_skip_layers.label: High-noise SLG Skip Layers
high_noise_skip_layers.description: SLG skip layers for the high-noise expert phase.

high_noise_slg_start.label: High-noise SLG Start
high_noise_slg_start.description: SLG start fraction during the high-noise phase.

high_noise_slg_end.label: High-noise SLG End
high_noise_slg_end.description: SLG end fraction during the high-noise phase.

moe_boundary.label: MoE Expert Boundary
moe_boundary.description: For Wan 2.2 MoE: fraction of total steps at which to switch from high-noise to low-noise expert. 0 = use `high_noise_steps` directly.

end_image_base64.label: End Image (base64)
end_image_base64.description: Optional final-frame target for Wan 2.2 — forces the video to end on this image. Lets you bridge two stills with generated motion.

control_frames.label: ControlNet Frames
control_frames.description: Per-frame control images for video generation. Length should match `video_frames` for full coverage.

vace_strength.label: VACE Strength
vace_strength.description: Strength of the VACE module (Wan 2.x video editing). 0 = disabled.

upscale_factor.label: Upscale Factor
upscale_factor.description: Target upscale factor. 4 = 4× the source image (default for ESRGAN).
upscale_factor.recommended: Match the loaded upscaler's native factor (4 for most ESRGAN variants).

tile_size.label: Upscale Tile Size
tile_size.description: Tile size for streaming the source image through the upscaler. Smaller = less VRAM, more boundary artifacts. 0 = whole-image.
tile_size.recommended: 128 default. Drop to 64 if hitting VRAM limits.

repeats.label: Upscale Repeats
repeats.description: Run the upscaler N times. With a 4× model: 1 pass = 4×, 2 passes = 16×.

job_id.label: Source Job ID
job_id.description: Convenience: use the output of an existing completed job as the source for upscaling, instead of providing `image_base64`. handle_upscale loads `outputs[image_index]` from disk, base64-encodes it, and substitutes `image_base64` before the worker runs — so to the worker the two paths are indistinguishable.
job_id.recommended: Use this when chaining 'generate, then upscale' from a queued job.

image_index.label: Source Image Index
image_index.description: Which output of the source job to upscale. Used with `job_id` when the source job produced multiple images (e.g. batch_count > 1). Ignored when `job_id` is absent. 0-based: 0 = first output.
image_index.recommended: Leave at 0 unless the source job had batch_count > 1 and you want a specific image.
